none: The upgrading of 103rd Street and Timuquana Road to a multi-lane facility, Jacksonville : environmental impact statement.

The project involves upgrading 103rd Street and Timuquana Road in Jacksonville, Florida, from a two-lane road to a multi-lane facility. This is intended to accommodate increasing traffic demands and improve safety. The development will follow the existing road alignment with some new sections, impacting local businesses, residences, and natural habitats. Concerns include increased noise, displacement of residents, and potential water pollution in Fishing Creek. The project aims to enhance connectivity and support regional growth, but it also raises environmental and community concerns.

Florida Game and Fresh Water Fish Commission

The project to upgrade 103rd Street and Timuquana Road in Jacksonville was proposed to address growing traffic demands and enhance safety in the area. However, it faced public pushback due to concerns over noise, displacement of residents, and potential environmental impacts on local habitats and water quality. Ultimately, the project proceeded, balancing infrastructure needs with community and environmental considerations.
"Several residents present at the hearing were concerned with the growing impact of noise on their residential neighborhoods." — Public Hearing, June 20, 1974
"The proposed fill would eliminate portions of vegetated flood plain which function in maintaining water quality and provide wildlife habitat." — Florida Department of Natural Resources
